ORTHOPAEDIC CONSULTATION NOTE

CHIEF COMPLAINT: Right knee pain and functional limitation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
This is a 67-year-old male presenting with progressive right knee pain over the past
3 years, now significantly limiting daily activities. Pain rated 8/10 with ambulation.
Unable to walk more than one block. Night pain disturbing sleep 4-5 nights per week.

IMAGING:
Weight-bearing AP and lateral radiographs demonstrate Kellgren-Lawrence Grade IV changes
with complete loss of medial joint space, subchondral sclerosis, large osteophytes,
and 12-degree varus malalignment.

DIAGNOSIS: Primary osteoarthritis, right knee (ICD-10: M17.11)

CONSERVATIVE TREATMENT HISTORY:
1. NSAIDs (Meloxicam 15mg daily) - 8 months - inadequate pain relief
2. Physical therapy (3x/week, 8 weeks) - completed without significant improvement
3. Cortisone injection (40mg triamcinolone acetonide) - temporary relief x 3 weeks
4. Hyaluronic acid viscosupplementation (Synvisc-One) - no meaningful benefit
5. Unloader brace - worn 6 months, minimal benefit

RECOMMENDATION:
Given failure of all reasonable conservative measures and severity of radiographic
findings with significant functional limitation, I recommend proceeding with right
total knee arthroplasty using the Stryker Triathlon Total Knee System.

Mako robotic-assisted surgical technique is indicated to optimize implant positioning
and achieve precise ligament balance, particularly important given the significant
varus deformity. Literature supports improved accuracy in component positioning with
robotic assistance in cases with significant pre-operative deformity.

PLAN:
Schedule right total knee arthroplasty with Mako robotic assistance.
Pre-operative medical clearance and CT scan for Mako planning.
